title: Lumora "Dewdrop" lip-oil color-variant set: cut out on white, recolor one hero shot into 8 shade variants + spec sheet

source.reference: Blind Box Packaging Design (PART A — Product Photo Retouching)

grounding_note: Grounded in the Upwork hourly brief "Blind Box Packaging Design", PART A — PRODUCT PHOTO RETOUCHING. VERBATIM phrases quoted from the brief: "Clean up iPhone product photos I'll provide of one physical sample"; "Maintain consistent lighting, shadow, and gold-clasp detail across all"; "internal highlights and shadows should shift naturally with each color". The brief also asks to "Cut out the case on a transparent background, 300 DPI" and to "Recolor the case + wrist strap into 11 additional color variants (12 total)" — the identical real-world intent kept here (one physical sample shot on a phone, cut out clean on a neutral background, then recolored into a full range with consistent lighting/shadow and translucent-material highlights preserved), with the count shifted to a realistic 8-shade cosmetics range and a metallic collar detail held constant across every variant. The original brief bundles this photo-retouch job with an out-of-scope Illustrator dieline rebuild (PART B, from-scratch vector packaging on a manufacturer's dieline); PART B is dropped because from-scratch vector layout composition is not in connector scope. FICTIONAL / IP-CLEAN: the real "Mogano Jewelry & Accessories" jewelry-case brand is replaced with a fictional beauty-cosmetics brand, Lumora Beauty Co., and its "Dewdrop" glass-bottle lip oil; the translucent-gemstone material becomes translucent lip oil in frosted glass, and the gold clasp becomes a brushed-gold collar and cap. The Adobe chain does the genuinely-doable work: straighten + tone + white-balance + crop the raw phone shot, cut the bottle onto a clean white e-commerce background, isolate the translucent oil/glass region, and re-hue it per shade using selection + HSL / single-color-saturation / color-overlay while masking the gold collar and cap out of the recolor so they stay constant.
one_line_ask: Clean up and cut out one raw phone shot of the Dewdrop lip-oil bottle onto a white e-commerce background, then recolor the translucent oil into 8 on-brand shade variants while keeping the metallic collar, cap, and lighting/shadow identical across all of them, and emit a variant spec sheet.
full_brief: Lumora Beauty Co. is launching "Dewdrop," a glossy translucent lip oil in a frosted-glass dropper bottle with a brushed-gold collar and cap. We only had time to photograph ONE physical sample on an iPhone against a messy studio bench, so we need you to turn that single raw shot into a full, launch-ready shade range for our Shopify PDP and Amazon listing. First, clean up the raw photo: it's slightly tilted, a touch cool/dull, and the background is cluttered. Straighten it, correct the tone and white balance so the glass reads clean and neutral, and crop it square to our 2000x2000 px listing frame. Then cut the bottle out cleanly and place it on a pure white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background — Amazon-compliant, no drop-shadow baked in, product centered with a little margin. Next, the important part: recolor JUST the translucent oil inside the bottle into 8 shade variants for the range — Clear Gloss (the original, ungraded), Peach Nectar, Rose Quartz, Coral Crush, Berry Tint, Amber Glow, Mauve Mist, and Cherry Sheer. The tint must look like real translucent oil: internal highlights and the light passing through the glass should shift naturally with each color, NOT a flat paint fill. Critically, the brushed-gold collar, the gold cap, the dropper glass rim, and the white background/shadow must stay IDENTICAL across all 8 — only the oil changes color. Deliverables: the 8 shade variants each as a 2000x2000 px white-background JPEG PLUS a transparent-background PNG at 300 DPI (16 product files total), and a one-page variant spec sheet (PDF) listing each shade name and its hue so our packaging team can match Pantone later. Keep everything photographic and consistent — this is a color-variant set of one product, not eight different photos.

inputs.name: dewdrop_raw_iphone.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal iPhone snapshot of a single frosted-glass dropper bottle of lip oil standing upright on a cluttered studio bench. The bottle has a brushed-gold metal collar and a gold dropper cap; inside is a clear/pale translucent oil with visible internal highlights and light passing through the frosted glass. Background is a messy makeup bench with brushes, a palette and a cloth, slightly out of focus but distracting. Slightly tilted horizon (about 3 degrees), flat dull lighting, slightly cool/blue white balance, real glass reflections and condensation, candid documentary look, no studio gloss, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: One physical sample shot on a phone, deliberately imperfect (tilt, cool/dull WB, cluttered background) so the cleanup + cutout + recolor chain has genuine work to do. The translucent oil + gold collar are the two regions the recolor must treat differently. gpt-image-2 for photoreal product.

inputs.name: lumora_shade_palette.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns shade_name,hue_target,saturation_shift,description for 8 lip-oil shades: Clear Gloss (original, no recolor), Peach Nectar (warm peach), Rose Quartz (soft pink), Coral Crush (coral-orange), Berry Tint (deep berry), Amber Glow (warm amber), Mauve Mist (muted mauve), Cherry Sheer (translucent red). hue_target as approximate HSL hue degrees, saturation_shift as a small +/- percentage, description as a one-line marketing note. These are the target hues the recolor steps aim for and the rows the spec sheet lists.
inputs.realism_notes: The client's own shade brief as a data handoff; drives which hue each recolor variant targets and populates the spec-sheet output. Clear Gloss row is flagged as the ungraded original so it is passed through, not recolored.

inputs.name: lumora_brandkit.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: A simple one-page brand kit sheet for a fictional beauty brand 'Lumora Beauty Co.': brand name in a light modern serif, a note that product hero images sit on pure white #FFFFFF at 2000x2000 px, brushed-gold accent color swatch (#C9A24B), instruction that metallic collar and cap must stay unchanged across shade variants, and a small footer 'Dewdrop lip oil — launch range'. Clean flat layout, cream and gold, print-like PDF page.
inputs.realism_notes: Reference-only brand constraints (white-bg spec, the gold accent that must be preserved, the 2000x2000 frame). Inspected during the job to confirm background color and which elements stay constant; not itself edited.

outputs.name: dewdrop_variants_white_x8.jpg
outputs.spec: Eight 2000x2000 px square JPEGs, one per shade (Clear Gloss, Peach Nectar, Rose Quartz, Coral Crush, Berry Tint, Amber Glow, Mauve Mist, Cherry Sheer), product centered on pure white #FFFFFF, identical framing/lighting/shadow and identical gold collar+cap across all eight; only the translucent oil color differs. Amazon/Shopify PDP-ready.

outputs.name: dewdrop_variants_transparent_x8.png
outputs.spec: The same eight shade variants each as a transparent-background PNG at 300 DPI for compositing into lifestyle/banner layouts; gold collar+cap preserved, only oil recolored.

outputs.name: dewdrop_variant_spec_sheet.pdf
outputs.spec: One-page variant spec sheet (recipe note rendered/converted to PDF) listing each of the 8 shade names, its target hue, and the constant-elements note (gold collar/cap unchanged, white #FFFFFF background) for the packaging team's later Pantone match.

connector_workflow.note: Inspect the supplied brand kit to confirm the white #FFFFFF background spec, the 2000x2000 frame, and which metallic elements (gold collar/cap) must stay constant across variants.
Level the ~3-degree tilt so the bottle stands vertical before any cutout or recolor.
Auto-tone to fix the flat/dull exposure so the translucent oil and glass read cleanly.
Neutralize the cool/blue white balance so the frosted glass and gold collar read true-to-life (baseline color before any tinting).
Crop and resize to the 2000x2000 px square listing frame with the bottle centered and margin, per the brand kit.
Cut the bottle out of the cluttered bench onto a transparent background (clean e-commerce cutout, the base for both white-bg and PNG deliverables).
Solid-fill the transparent area with pure white #FFFFFF to produce the Amazon-compliant white-background base (SOLID fill only, no synthesized content).

Select just the translucent oil inside the glass (prompt: 'the liquid oil inside the bottle'), excluding the gold collar, cap and glass rim — this mask is reused to constrain every recolor so the metal stays constant.

Peach Nectar: shift the hue of the selected oil region toward the CSV peach hue; internal highlights ride along so it reads as tinted translucent oil, not flat paint. Gold collar/cap outside the mask untouched.
Rose Quartz: re-hue the same masked oil region toward the soft-pink CSV target on the clean base; metal and background held constant.
Boost only the recolored pink oil's saturation to hit the Rose Quartz saturation_shift from the CSV without touching the gold or white.
Coral Crush: re-hue the masked oil toward coral-orange on the clean base; highlights preserved for the translucent look.
Berry Tint: shift the masked oil to a deep berry hue; because it's darker, keep exposure of the highlights so the glass still reads translucent.
Amber Glow: warm the masked oil toward amber; a natural sibling of the neutral base so the gold collar still coordinates.
Mauve Mist: apply a muted-mauve color overlay confined to the oil mask (overlay blends with the underlying highlights so it stays translucent, not opaque); metal/background untouched.
Cherry Sheer: re-hue the masked oil to a translucent red; keeps the sheer highlights so it reads as a see-through tint over the dropper.
Regenerate the transparent-PNG deliverable for the Peach variant from its white-bg version (representative of the batch; each white variant is cut to a matching 300 DPI transparent PNG).
Clear Gloss variant: the ungraded original oil is passed through unchanged (per the CSV 'no recolor' row); re-affirm the 2000x2000 white-bg frame so it sits in the set as variant #1.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: lumora_shade_palette.csv, variant_peach_white, variant_rose_white_final, variant_coral_white, variant_berry_white, variant_amber_white, variant_mauve_white, variant_cherry_white, clear_gloss_white_final
connector_workflow.note: Write the variant spec: each of the 8 shade names, its applied hue/saturation, and the constant-elements note (gold collar/cap unchanged, white #FFFFFF background) for the packaging team's later Pantone match.
Convert the recipe note into the one-page variant spec-sheet PDF deliverable.
Create the delivery folder structure (white-bg JPEGs, transparent PNGs, spec sheet) in Creative Cloud storage.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: clear_gloss_white_final, variant_peach_white, variant_rose_white_final, variant_coral_white, variant_berry_white, variant_amber_white, variant_mauve_white, variant_cherry_white, variant_peach_png, dewdrop_variant_spec_sheet.pdf, delivery_folder
connector_workflow.note: Initialize the upload of all 8 white-bg variants, the transparent PNG(s), and the spec sheet into the delivery folder.
Finalize the upload; returns the shareable asset URNs for the full Dewdrop variant set handed to the client.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining. A single base-prep spine feeds everything: dewdrop_raw_iphone.jpg -> auto_straighten(2) -> auto_tone(3) -> color_temperature(4) -> crop_resize(5) -> remove_background(6) -> fill_area white(7), yielding bottle_on_white_base, the clean neutral hero every variant is built from. From that base, select_by_prompt(8) produces oil_region_mask, the single reusable selection that constrains every recolor so the gold collar/cap and white background never change. Each shade variant (9,10,12,13,14,15,16) consumes the SAME bottle_on_white_base + oil_region_mask + the shade-palette CSV and recolors only the masked oil region -- a fan-out of parallel recolors off one clean base, exactly how a real color-variant set is produced (build once, re-hue many). Rose adds a dependent saturation pass (10 -> 11). The transparent-PNG deliverable branches off a finished white variant (variant_peach_white -> remove_background 17). Clear Gloss (18) is the pass-through original off the same base. All eight finished variants + the CSV converge into the recipe note(19) -> convert_pdf(20) spec sheet, and every finished deliverable plus the folder(21) converges into the upload session(22) -> finalize(23). No step stands free of the chain; the variant set emerges only from the assembled base-prep-then-fan-out pipeline.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon product-recolor task: 23 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe-server tools, well above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor. Difficulty is in the discipline of a real color-variant job: prep one raw phone shot through a 6-step cleanup+cutout spine, fill to a compliant white background, derive ONE reusable oil-only mask via select_by_prompt, then fan that mask out across eight parallel recolors using hue-shift / single-color-saturation / color-overlay so the translucent oil changes shade while the gold collar, cap and white background stay pixel-identical -- the exact 'maintain consistent lighting, shadow, and metallic detail across all variants; translucent material highlights shift naturally' constraint from the source brief. It then produces dual deliverable formats (white JPEG + transparent PNG), a data-driven spec sheet, and a clean CC delivery folder. It respects every hard limit: image_fill_area is SOLID white fill only (no synthesized content); image_remove_background outputs only transparent PNGs; recoloring is hue/saturation/overlay manipulation of EXISTING pixels within a selection, never generative fill, never inpainting/object-removal, never text-to-image or upscaling; nothing is composed from scratch.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used: no image_crop_and_resize / expand / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo; no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting of occluded pixels; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external accounts. All recoloring is hue/saturation/overlay transformation of EXISTING pixels inside a selection mask.
reverify.notes: Verified against the connector palette and slot R06. FEASIBILITY: every step uses only allowed tools. (1) image_fill_area (n7) is SOLID #FFFFFF fill of the already-transparent margin only -- it synthesizes no product content, just the compliant white e-commerce background behind the cut-out bottle. (2) image_remove_background (n6, n17) only ever produces a transparent PNG cutout of an existing subject -- no reconstruction of hidden pixels. (3) The recolors (n9,n10,n12-n16) are pure color manipulation (image_adjust_hsl / image_adjust_single_color_saturation / image_apply_color_overlay) applied within an image_select_by_prompt mask isolating the translucent oil; the gold collar/cap and background are outside the mask and unchanged -- exactly the source brief's 'recolor into variants; keep lighting/shadow/metallic detail consistent' and standard non-generative recolor work. (4) image_select_by_prompt (n8) only produces a selection mask, no generation. (5) local_write_recipe_note (n19) + document_convert_pdf (n20) emit the spec-sheet data/PDF, no design composition. Layout/render tools that would compose a from-scratch layout are intentionally NOT used, so no from-scratch design. COUNTS: 23 connector_workflow steps -> tool_call_count=23; 15 distinct adobe-server tools -> distinct_adobe_tools=15 (local_write_recipe_note is [L], not counted). GROUNDING: grounding_note quotes verbatim substrings confirmed present in the candidate brief ('Clean up iPhone product photos I'll provide of one physical sample'; 'Maintain consistent lighting, shadow, and gold-clasp detail across all'; 'internal highlights and shadows should shift naturally with each color'). SLOT-MATCH: category=product_ecom_whitebg, operation O2, family Photo & Image, output_modality image, workflow_nature edit, vertical beauty_cosmetics -- all equal the R06 slot; the fictional IP-clean brand Lumora Beauty Co. / Dewdrop replaces the real Mogano jewelry brand. This is a GEN-FREE task consistent with the slot's replacement_note (no generative-expand).
